title: Podman Does Not Use Quadlet Files in System Search Path on Raspberry Pi 3+
body:
### Issue Description

Attempting to use Quadlet to create a Cockpit container to manage the host with this file in `/etc/containers/systemd/cockpit.container`

```
[Unit]
Description=Cockpit Container
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Container]
Image=quay.io/cockpit/ws:latest
ContainerName=cockpit-ws
Environment=NAME=cockpit-ws

#flag for autoupdates
Label=io.containers.autoupdate=registry

Volume=/:/host
PodmanArgs=--pid host --privileged
Exec=/container/label-run

[Service]
Restart=always

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create cockpit.container file in appropriate rootful podman quadlet search path
2. Systemctl daemon-reload
3. No systemd service file created.


### Describe the results you received

Running a `sudo systemctl daemon-reload` and `sudo sytemctl status cockpit` gives me: `Unit cockpit.service could not be found.`

### Describe the results you expected

cockpit.service should be found and created.

### podman info output

```yaml

  kernel: 6.1.0-rpi7-rpi-v8

  ociRuntime:
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

comments.body:
Hi, from the output of `podman system info` I can see that you are running version 4.3.1 of Podman:
```
version:
  APIVersion: 4.3.1
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.19.8
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/arm64
  Version: 4.3.1
```
However, Quadlet was first released as part of version 4.4

Could you see if quadlet is complaining about a bad quadlet file.

/usr/libexec/podman/quadlet --dryrun

> Could you see if quadlet is complaining about a bad quadlet file.
> 
> /usr/libexec/podman/quadlet --dryrun

@rhatdan Podman v4.3.1 does not include Quadlet
